title: DSP Result Envelope
description: Layer 3: The structured output produced by a processing agent inside an enclave. This is the ONLY artifact that crosses from the customer's infrastructure to the outside world. It MUST contain structured extractions only — never raw document content, images, full text, or unredacted PII.

result_id.description: Unique identifier for this result. Prefix: 'rs-'.

contract_id.description: The processing contract under which this result was produced.

session_id.description: The processing session that produced this result. Prefix: 'ps-'.

attestation.description: Cryptographic proof that this result was produced inside an attested enclave.

signature.description: Signature over the entire result envelope (excluding this signature field).

network_egress_bytes.description: Total bytes sent from the enclave. Should match the result envelope size.

network_destinations.description: All network destinations contacted during processing. Must match contract's allowed_destinations.

input_document_hashes.description: Hashes of all documents read during processing. Proves exactly which documents were accessed.

output_result_hash.description: Hash of the entire result envelope (excluding attestation.signature). Allows third parties to verify integrity.

sub_agent_chain.description: Ordered list of sub-agents/models used during processing. REQUIRED when the primary agent delegates to sub-models (e.g., OCR → layout → extraction → validation). Each entry declares the sub-agent's hash, purpose, and whether it ran in the same enclave. Enables the owner to verify the full multi-model pipeline, not just the orchestrator.

extractions.description: Structured data extracted from each processed document.

pii_report.description: Mandatory report on how PII was handled during this processing session.

result_scan.description: REQUIRED. Report from the result scanning phase. A separate, audited process that checks result content for PII before it exits the boundary. This is the primary defense against LLM free-text leakage.

performed.description: Whether result scanning was actually executed. MUST be true for llm_freeform agents.

verdicts.description: Verdicts from each scanner that ran. Multiple scanners may run (regex + NER + statistical).

overall_passed.description: true only if ALL scanners passed. If any scanner failed, the result SHOULD NOT have been delivered (depending on contract.scan_failure_action).

fields_modified_by_scan.description: Number of fields that were redacted or modified by the scanning phase (not by the agent's own redaction).

scan_duration_ms.description: Time spent in the scanning phase. Separate from agent processing time.

end_of_session_attestation.description: Fresh attestation produced at session end. Proves the enclave was not compromised during processing. Separate from the initial attestation at session start.

measurement.description: Enclave measurement at session end. MUST match the start measurement if the enclave was not tampered with.

measurement_matches_start.description: true if the end measurement matches the start measurement. false indicates potential compromise.

errors.description: Non-fatal errors encountered during processing. Fatal errors prevent result generation entirely.

source_document_hash.description: Hash of the document at time of processing. Proves which version was read.

extraction_type.description: What type of extraction was performed (may differ from document classification if auto-detected).

confidence.description: Overall confidence score for this extraction. 1.0 = highest confidence.

fields.description: Key-value extracted fields. Values MUST comply with the contract's pii_redaction_rules. No raw text blocks.

tables.description: Structured tabular data extracted from the document.

classifications.description: Document classification results (if classify_document was requested).

validation.description: Self-validation results: did the extraction pass internal consistency checks?

pii_field.description: If this column contains PII, which type. Redaction rules from the contract apply.

pii_bearing.description: If true, this column MAY contain embedded PII in free-text (e.g., transaction descriptions with counterparty names). Values in pii_bearing columns MUST be scanned by NER and redacted. Default false. MUST be set to true for 'description', 'memo', 'notes', 'reference' columns in financial documents.

rows.description: Row data. Each row is an array of values matching the columns definition. PII fields MUST be redacted per contract rules.

checksum.description: Hash of the serialized table data for integrity verification.

passed.description: Whether all internal validation checks passed.

fields_encountered.description: All PII field types detected during processing.

fields_redacted.description: PII fields that were redacted in the output.

redaction_methods_applied.description: Map of PIIFieldType → RedactionMethod actually applied.

fields_allowed_by_contract.description: PII fields that were passed through because the contract explicitly allows them.

raw_content_included.description: MUST be false unless the contract explicitly sets raw_content_allowed: true.

compliance_status.description: 'compliant': all redaction rules were followed. 'violation_detected': agent detected it could not fully comply (result MAY still be produced with best-effort redaction). 'unknown': unable to determine compliance.

violations.description: Details of any compliance violations. Present only when compliance_status is 'violation_detected'.

message.description: Human-readable error description. MUST NOT contain document content or PII.

document_id.description: Which document caused the error, if applicable.
